name: simutil
description: It's not just a cross-platform TUI app that quickly opens Android & iOS emulators/simulators
readme_summary: SimUtil is a cross-platform terminal UI application for launching, managing, and connecting to Android emulators and iOS simulators. It provides a streamlined interface within the terminal to interact with virtual devices, offering features like one-key launch, custom launch options, and wireless connectivity to physical devices.
key_features: One-Key Launch for emulators/simulators
Custom Android launch options
Shutdown devices
Logcat viewing with filtering
Wireless connection to physical Android devices (ADB Tools)
Custom plugin system for external tools (scrcpy, Maestro, etc.)
Edit configuration file directly from the app
primary_use_case: To provide a fast, terminal-based interface for developers to manage and launch Android emulators and iOS simulators, improving workflow efficiency.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot character standing at a futuristic control panel within a dimly lit terminal environment. The robot is using a glowing holographic joystick to launch a fleet of miniature, stylized Android robots and iOS-themed rockets into the air. Small, abstract data streams and code snippets flow around the control panel, subtly indicating the technical nature of the operation. The overall mood is efficient and powerful, with a focus on the ease of launching multiple devices simultaneously. The repository name 'SimUtil' is subtly integrated into the design of the control panel. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a vibrant, contrasting color palette.

name: tabwrangler
description: A browser extension that automatically closes your unused tabs so you can focus on the tabs that matter
readme_summary: Tab Wrangler is a browser extension for Chrome and Firefox that automatically closes unused tabs to improve browser performance and reduce clutter. It provides features to manage closed tabs, prevent specific tabs from closing, and exclude certain websites.
key_features: Automatic tab closing
Tab Corral for managing and restoring closed tabs
Full session restore with browsing history
Tab Lock to prevent auto-closing
Website exclusion list
Protection for tabs playing audio or in tab groups
Minimum open tabs setting
Right-click menu integration
Pause and resume functionality
Protection for pinned and active tabs
primary_use_case: To declutter browser tabs, improve performance by closing inactive tabs, and provide easy restoration of closed tabs.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian diligently organizing a vast, chaotic library of open books. The librarian gently places unused books onto a 'closed' shelf, each with a small, glowing bookmark indicating its content. A few select books are marked with a golden lock, signifying they are important and must remain accessible. The library itself is clean and well-lit, with subtle digital particles floating in the air, representing data. The librarian's desk has a small, minimalist screen showing the 'Tab Wrangler' logo and a count of 'organized' tabs. The scene should evoke a sense of calm and order amidst digital information. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, friendly aesthetic and a warm color palette.

name: iOS
readme_summary: This repository provides the official Home Assistant client application for Apple platforms, allowing users to control and monitor their smart home devices from their iPhones, iPads, and Macs. It offers a native experience for managing automations, scenes, and devices.
key_features: Native iOS, iPadOS, and macOS applications for Home Assistant
Control and monitor smart home devices
Manage automations and scenes
TestFlight beta program for early access
Integration with Apple's developer tools for building and testing
primary_use_case: To provide a dedicated, native mobile and desktop application for users to interact with their Home Assistant smart home setup on Apple devices.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot assistant holding a glowing orb representing a smart home. The robot is standing in a cozy living room, and from the orb, holographic projections of various smart home devices (lights, thermostat, locks) are emanating. Subtle lines of code or data streams flow from the orb to the robot, indicating control and monitoring. The overall mood is helpful and intuitive, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft lighting and a vibrant color palette, emphasizing ease of use and comprehensive control.

name: IPBan
description: Since 2011, IPBan is the worlds most trusted, free security software to block hackers and botnets. With both Windows and Linux support, IPBan has your dedicated or cloud server protected. Upgrade to IPBan Pro today and get a discount. Learn more at ↓
readme_summary: IPBan is a free, open-source security software for Windows and Linux that automatically blocks IP addresses of attackers attempting to gain unauthorized access to servers. It monitors event logs and application logs for failed login attempts and integrates with firewalls to ban malicious IPs, protecting servers from brute-force attacks and botnets.
key_features: Automatic IP banning based on failed login attempts
Support for Windows and Linux platforms
Monitors event viewer and log files for security events
Configurable ban thresholds and durations
Supports IPv4 and IPv6
Easy unbanning via unban.txt file
Integration with firewalls (firewalld, nftables, iptables)
Extensible to monitor various applications (RDP, SSH, MySQL, etc.)
primary_use_case: Protecting servers (dedicated or cloud) on Windows and Linux from unauthorized access attempts by automatically blocking malicious IP addresses.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant digital knight standing guard before a server rack, holding a shield that displays a list of blocked IP addresses. The knight's sword is actively deflecting incoming digital arrows, each labeled with 'failed login'. Successful connections are represented by gentle, glowing streams of data passing safely. Subtle UI elements showing IPBan's configuration and a small 'blocked' icon are visible on the knight's armor. The background is a dark, secure data center with a soft, protective blue aura. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a focus on the protective action.
